rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no fixed retrospectives

rules_evaluated.evidence: 1-65 | Student-facing body uses direct, peer-warm Sutherland + Siilasmaa register; no consultancy pap

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: 23:23 | 'In Claude Code Desktop, click the **+** next to the prompt, then **Settings → Connectors**. In Cowork, open **Customize → Connectors**. Look for Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Confluence, SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, or the system your company actually uses.' — 'Connector' appears as a UI label and path element before functional earning
rules_evaluated.fix: Add one-breath primer on first use: 'Look for **connectors** — connections to places where your work lives (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Confluence, SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, or the system your company actually uses).' Then in subsequent mentions, the term is established.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream
rules_evaluated.evidence: 27:31 | Punchy line 'In Claude Code, that control point is **plan mode**' followed by clear structural carve-out via runtime-fork divs; no softening or over-hedging

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT in student-facing body
rules_evaluated.evidence: No defensive positioning vocabulary ('not aspirational', 'not vendor', 'unlike consultancy', 'grounded in actual', 'real practitioners') detected in student-facing prose

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: 23:23 | 'Connector' appears in UI path ('Settings → Connectors') before functional context establishes what a connector IS. Student at prework stage does not yet have Claude Code opened; the term lands cold.
rules_evaluated.fix: Earn the term one breath before naming the UI path: 'Look for **connectors** — connections to places where your work lives (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Confluence, SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, or the system your company actually uses). In Claude Code Desktop, click the **+** next to the prompt, then **Settings → Connectors**.'

rules_evaluated.evidence: 5:9 | 'What you do' framing names what the student will bring and learn, not what they're missing

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: 23:23 | 'Connector' used as UI label and path reference before earning
rules_evaluated.fix: See check_writing.md rule 14 fix above — same violation.

rules_evaluated.evidence: 60:62 | 'memory can start from files, pasted excerpts, and the sources you bring into the training directory' — correct usage of memory as persistent artifact

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in the body

rules_evaluated.evidence: 11:62 | 'Bring one live challenge', 'Check what Claude can read', 'Learn plan mode', 'Read the memory frame' — all imperative verbs

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never filename
rules_evaluated.evidence: 39,40,48,49 | All links use descriptive titles ('Claude quick reference', 'I was an AI skeptic...') not filenames
